name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ذِكْرًا كَثِيرًا (٤١)
```

```english
O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance, (41)
```

```arabic
وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا (٤٢)
```

```english
and glorify Him morning and evening. (42)
```

```arabic
هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُصَلِّى عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتُهُۥ لِيُخْرِجَكُم مِّنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۚ وَكَانَ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيمًا (٤٣)
```

```english
It is He who blesses you—and so do His angels—that He may bring you out from darkness into light, and He is most merciful to the faithful. (43)
```

```arabic
تَحِيَّتُهُمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُۥ سَلَـٰمٌ ۚ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا كَرِيمًا (٤٤)
```

```english
The day they encounter Him, their greeting will be, ‘Peace,’ and He holds in store for them a noble reward. (44)
```

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ شَـٰهِدًا وَمُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا (٤٥)
```

```english
O Prophet! Indeed We have sent you as a witness, as a bearer of good news and as a warner (45)
```

```arabic
وَدَاعِيًا إِلَى ٱللَّهِ بِإِذْنِهِۦ وَسِرَاجًا مُّنِيرًا (٤٦)
```

```english
and as a summoner to Allah by His permission, and as a radiant lamp. (46)
```

```arabic
وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ فَضْلًا كَبِيرًا (٤٧)
```

```english
Announce to the faithful the good news that there will be for them a great grace from Allah. (47)
```

```arabic
وَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَدَعْ أَذَىٰهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا (٤٨)
```

```english
Do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites, and disregard their torments, and put your trust in Allah, and Allah suffices as trustee. (48)
```

## (Commentary)

These are verses that call the believers to remembrance (الذكر) and glorification (التسبيح), giving them glad tidings and promising them a beautiful promise. They address the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) by his noble attributes, commanding him to give glad tidings to the believers and not to obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites. It is possible that these two groups of verses were revealed at different times.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً** "O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance" (33:41). Remembrance (الذكر) — *the state of consciousness that stands in contrast to forgetfulness* — is the directing of perception toward the One remembered. As for the verbal utterance of His names and attributes that point toward Him, it is merely one of the manifestations of remembrance.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً** "and glorify Him morning and evening" (33:42). Glorification (التسبيح) — *the act of declaring Allah free from any deficiency or likeness to creation* — is a form of hallowing (التنزيه). Like remembrance, it does not depend solely on verbal utterance, even if the pronouncing of phrases such as "Glory be to Allah" (سبحان الله) is one of its manifestations.

The morning (البكرة) refers to the beginning of the day, and the evening (الأصيل) refers to its end after the afternoon (العصر). The restriction of glorification to the morning and evening is due to the transformation of states that occurs during these times; thus, it is appropriate to glorify and hallow Him from change, transformation, and every occurring deficiency. It is also possible that "morning and evening" together serve as a metaphor for continuity, similar to "night and day" in His saying: **يُسَبِّحُونَ لَهُ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ** "they glorify Him night and day" (41:38).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلائِكَتُهُ لِيُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ** "It is He who blesses you, and His angels [as well], that He may bring you out from darkness into light" (33:43). The comprehensive meaning of "blessing" or "prayer" (الصلاة), as gleaned from its various usages, is "inclination" (الانعطاف). Thus, its meaning varies according to that to which it is attributed. For this reason, it has been said: blessing from Allah is mercy (الرحمة), from the angels it is seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار), and from people it is supplication (الدعاء). However, that which is attributed to Allah, the Glorified, in the Quran as "blessing" is the mercy specific to the believers—that which results in the felicity of the Hereafter and eternal success. This is why He justified His blessing upon them by saying: **لِيُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَكانَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيماً** "that He may bring you out from darkness into light; and He is most merciful to the faithful" (33:43).

In His discourse, the Glorified has linked their forgetting Him to His forgetting them, and their remembering Him to His remembering them. He said: **نَسُوا اللهَ فَنَسِيَهُمْ** "They forgot Allah, so He forgot them" (9:67), and He said: **فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ** "Remember Me, and I will remember you" (2:152). His blessing upon them is His remembrance of them through mercy. If they remember Him frequently and glorify Him morning and evening, He blesses them frequently, envelops them in light, and distances them from darkness.

From this, it becomes clear that His saying: **هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ** "It is He who blesses you..." serves as the justification for His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً** "O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance." The implication of this justification is that if you remember Allah frequently, He will remember you with His mercy frequently and will go to great lengths to bring you out of darkness into light. It can also be inferred that the "darkness" (الظلمات) is the darkness of forgetfulness and heedlessness, while the "light" (النور) is the light of remembrance.

In His saying: **وَكانَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيماً** "and He is most merciful to the faithful," the explicit noun "the faithful" (المؤمنين) is used in place of a pronoun (i.e., He did not say "and He is most merciful to you") to indicate that the cause of this mercy is the attribute of faith (الإيمان).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **تَحِيَّتُهمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُ سَلامٌ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْراً كَرِيماً** "Their greeting on the day they encounter Him will be, ‘Peace!’ and He has prepared for them a noble reward" (33:44). The apparent context suggests that "their greeting" (تحيتهم) is an infinitive (مصدر) added to the object; meaning, they will be greeted—in the passive sense—on the day they meet their Lord, by their Lord and His angels, with "Peace" (السلام). This means that on the day of the encounter, they will be in security and peace, where no harm shall befall them and no punishment shall touch them.

His saying: **وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْراً كَرِيماً** "and He has prepared for them a noble reward" means that Allah has readied for them a magnificent recompense.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ شاهِداً وَمُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً** "O Prophet! Indeed We have sent you as a witness, as a bearer of good news and as a warner" (33:45). His (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) being a "witness" (شاهداً) over deeds means that he bears witness to them in this worldly realm and will testify to them on the Day of Resurrection. It has been previously explained regarding His saying: **لِتَكُونُوا شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيداً** "that you may be witnesses to mankind, and that the Apostle may be a witness to you" (2:143) and other verses of witnessing, that he (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) is the witness over all witnesses.

His being a "bearer of good news" (مبشراً) and a "warner" (نذيرا) refers to his conveying glad tidings to the believers who obey Allah and His Messenger regarding Allah's reward and Paradise, and his warning the disbelievers and the disobedient regarding Allah's punishment and the Fire.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَداعِياً إِلَى اللهِ بِإِذْنِهِ وَسِراجاً مُنِيراً** "and as a summoner to Allah by His permission, and as a radiant lamp" (33:46). His "summoning to Allah" is his calling people to faith in Allah alone, which necessitates faith in the religion of Allah. Restricting the summons to being "by His permission" makes it synonymous with the divine mission (البعثة).

His (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) being a "radiant lamp" (سراجاً منيراً) means that he is the means by which people are guided to their felicity and saved from the darkness of wretchedness and misguidance. This is a metaphor (استعارة). The view of some who claim that the "radiant lamp" refers to the Quran—implying the meaning "possessor of a radiant lamp"—is an unnecessary affectation.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ فَضْلاً كَبِيراً** "And give good news to the faithful that there shall be for them a great bounty from Allah" (33:47). Grace or bounty (الفضل) refers to a bestowal that is given without the recipient having a prior right to it. Allah has described His bestowal by saying: **مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثالِها** "Whoever brings a good deed shall receive ten times its like" (6:160) and: **لَهُمْ ما يَشاؤُنَ فِيها وَلَدَيْنا مَزِيدٌ** "There they will have whatever they wish, and with Us there is yet more" (50:35). Thus, He clarified that He gives a reward that far exceeds the deed, and this is "grace." There is no evidence in the verse to suggest that this is restricted to the Hereafter.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلا تُطِعِ الْكافِرِينَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ** "And do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites, and disregard their annoyance, and put your trust in Allah..." (33:48). The meaning of "obeying the disbelievers and the hypocrites" was discussed at the beginning of the Surah.

His saying: **وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ** "and disregard their annoyance" means to leave aside the harm they cause you by turning away from it and not becoming preoccupied with it. The evidence for this meaning is His saying: **وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ** "and put your trust in Allah," meaning do not rely on yourself alone in repelling their harm, but rather make Allah your trustee (وكيلاً) in that matter, and "Allah suffices as a trustee."

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from Ibn al-Qaddah, from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: **ما من شيء إلا وله حد ينتهي إليه إلا الذكر ـ فليس له حد ينتهي إليه ـ فرض الله عز وجل الفرائض ـ فمن أداهن فهو حدهن ـ وشهر رمضان فمن صامه فهو حده ـ والحج فمن حج فهو حده إلا الذكر ـ فإن الله عز وجل لم يرض منه بالقليل ـ ولم يجعل له حدا ينتهي إليه ـ ثم تلا : « يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً ـ وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً » فقال : لم يجعل الله له حدا ينتهي إليه ـ.** — "There is nothing except that it has a limit where it ends, except for remembrance (الذكر)—for it has no limit where it ends. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, ordained the obligatory duties—so whoever performs them, that is their limit. And the month of Ramadan—whoever fasts it, that is its limit. And the Hajj—whoever performs the pilgrimage, that is its limit. Except for remembrance, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is not pleased with a small amount of it, and He did not set for it a limit where it ends." Then he recited: "O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance, and glorify Him morning and evening," and said: "Allah did not set for it a limit where it ends."

He said: **وكان أبي كثير الذكر ـ لقد كنت أمشي معه وإنه ليذكر الله وآكل معه الطعام ـ وإنه ليذكر الله ولقد كان يحدث القوم ما يشغله ذلك ـ عن ذكر الله وكنت أرى لسانا لازقا بحنكه يقول : لا إله إلا الله ـ.** — "My father [Imam al-Baqir] used to remember Allah frequently. I would walk with him and he would be remembering Allah; I would eat food with him and he would be remembering Allah. He would be speaking to people, yet that would not distract him from the remembrance of Allah. I would see his tongue clinging to his palate, saying: 'There is no god but Allah' (لا إله إلا الله)."

**وكان يجمعنا فيأمرنا بالذكر حتى تطلع الشمس ـ ويأمر بالقراءة من كان يقرأ منا ومن كان لا يقرأ منا أمره بالذكر ، والبيت الذي يقرأ فيه القرآن ويذكر الله عز وجل فيه يكثر بركته ويحضره الملائكة ويهجره الشياطين ـ ويضيء لأهل السماء كما يضيء الكوكب لأهل الأرض ـ والبيت الذي لا يقرأ فيه القرآن ـ ولا يذكر الله يقل بركته ويهجره الملائكة ويحضره الشياطين.** — "He used to gather us and command us to engage in remembrance until the sun rose. He would command those of us who could recite to recite [the Quran], and those who could not, he would command to engage in remembrance. The house in which the Quran is recited and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is remembered, its blessings increase, the angels attend it, and the devils abandon it; it shines for the inhabitants of heaven just as the stars shine for the inhabitants of earth. But the house in which the Quran is not recited and Allah is not remembered, its blessings decrease, the angels abandon it, and the devils attend it."

And the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: **ألا أخبركم بخير أعمالكم ـ أرفعها في درجاتكم وأزكاها عند مليككم ـ وخير لكم من الدينار والدرهم ـ وخير لكم من أن تلقوا عدوكم فتقتلوهم ويقتلوكم؟ فقالوا : بلى. قال : ذكر الله عز وجل كثيرا.** — "Shall I not inform you of the best of your deeds—the highest of them in your ranks, the purest of them before your Sovereign, better for you than gold and silver, and better for you than meeting your enemy such that you strike their necks and they strike yours?" They said: "Yes." He said: "Frequent remembrance of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic."

Then he said: **جاء رجل إلى النبي صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله فقال : من خير أهل المسجد؟ فقال : أكثرهم لله ذكرا.** — "A man came to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and asked: 'Who is the best of the people of the mosque?' He replied: 'The one among them who remembers Allah the most.'"

And the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: **من أعطي لسانا ذاكرا ـ فلقد أعطي خير الدنيا والآخرة.** — "Whoever is given a remembering tongue has indeed been given the good of this world and the Hereafter."

And he said regarding His saying: **وَلا تَمْنُنْ تَسْتَكْثِرُ** "and do not confer a favor to seek more" (74:6): **لا تستكثر ما عملت من خير لله.** — "Do not consider the good you have done for Allah to be much."

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from Abu al-Mu'azza in a *marfu‘* narration, he said: Imam ‘Ali (peace be upon him) said: **من ذكر الله في السر فقد ذكر الله كثيرا ـ إن المنافقين كانوا يذكرون الله علانية ـ ولا يذكرونه في السر فقال الله عز وجل : « يُراؤُنَ النَّاسَ وَلا يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ».** — "Whoever remembers Allah in secret has indeed remembered Allah frequently. Indeed, the hypocrites used to remember Allah publicly but would not remember Him in secret, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'they show off to the people and do not remember Allah except a little' (4:142)."

**I say:** This is a subtle and profound inference.

In *al-Khisal*, from Zayd al-Shahham, who said: Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: **ما ابتلي المؤمن بشيء أشد عليه من ثلاث خصال يحرمها. قيل : وما هي؟ قال : المواساة في ذات يده ، والإنصاف من نفسه ، وذكر الله كثيرا. أما إني لا أقول : سبحان الله والحمد لله ولا إله إلا الله والله أكبر وإن كان منه ـ ولكن ذكر الله عند ما أحل له وذكر الله عند ما حرم عليه.** — "A believer is not tested with anything harder for him than three qualities of which he may be deprived." It was asked: "And what are they?" He said: "Consoling others with his wealth, being just toward others against himself, and the frequent remembrance of Allah. Indeed, I do not mean [just] saying: 'Glory be to Allah, praise be to Allah, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is Great'—though that is part of it—but rather remembering Allah regarding what He has made lawful for him and remembering Allah regarding what He has forbidden him."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Bayhaqi narrated from Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri: The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) was asked: "Which of the servants is highest in rank before Allah on the Day of Resurrection?" He said: **الذاكرون الله كثيرا.** — "Those who remember Allah frequently." I said: "O Messenger of Allah, what about the warrior in the way of Allah?" He said: **لو ضرب بسيفه في الكفار والمشركين ـ حتى ينكسر ويختضب دما ـ لكان الذاكرون الله أفضل درجة منه.** — "Even if he were to strike with his sword among the disbelievers and polytheists until it breaks and he is dyed in blood, those who remember Allah would still be superior to him in rank."

In *al-‘Ilal*, with its chain of transmission from ‘Abdullah ibn al-Hasan, from his father, from his grandfather al-Hasan ibn ‘Ali (peace be upon him), who said: A group of Jews came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), and the most learned among them asked him several questions, among which he asked: "Why were you named Muhammad, Ahmad, Abu al-Qasim, Bashir (the bearer of glad tidings), Nadhir (the warner), and Da‘i (the summoner)?" He (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) replied: **أما الداعي فإني أدعو الناس إلى دين ربي عز وجل ، وأما النذير فإني أنذر بالنار من عصاني ، وأما البشير فإني أبشر بالجنة من أطاعني.** — "As for the Summoner, I summon people to the religion of my Lord, the Mighty and Majestic. As for the Warner, I warn with the Fire whoever disobeys me. As for the Bearer of Glad Tidings, I give glad tidings of Paradise to whoever obeys me." (The tradition continues).

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ** — up to His saying — **وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ وَكَفى بِاللهِ وَكِيلاً**, it is mentioned that it was revealed in Mecca five years before the Migration (الهجرة).
